title: Inviscid Incompressible Flow
summary: Potential flow, circulation, and the foundations of lift. Kutta-Joukowski theorem in action before viscosity enters the picture.
objectives: Solve Laplace equation for flow over cylinders

title: Oblique Shocks & Prandtl-Meyer Expansions
summary: When flow turns through a compression corner an oblique shock forms. Through an expansion corner a centered expansion fan (Prandtl-Meyer) smoothly accelerates and turns the flow.
objectives: Solve oblique shock properties for wedges
Compare normal vs oblique vs isentropic turning

crossConnections.description: Nozzle exit pressure (Sutton) determines shock/expansion losses in atmosphere (Anderson)

title: Boundary Layer Theory
summary: The thin viscous layer that makes or breaks aircraft and rockets. Laminar vs turbulent, separation, and skin friction drag.

keyEquations.description: Blasius flat plate boundary layer thickness

objectives: Estimate skin friction on a rocket body
Understand transition and its drag penalty
Predict separation on an adverse gradient

crossConnections.description: Induced drag is a major Δv loss that the propulsion system (Sutton) must overcome

title: Airfoils & Finite Wing Theory
summary: Thin airfoil theory gives the foundation for Cl(α) and the Kutta condition. Prandtl's lifting-line theory shows how finite aspect ratio creates induced drag and reduces effective angle of attack.

keyEquations.description: Ideal lift slope 2π per radian for thin airfoils

objectives: Apply thin airfoil + lifting line theory
See AR and taper effects on induced drag

title: Drag Polar & Performance
summary: The single most useful plot in aerodynamics: C_D vs C_L. How to read L/D max and its direct impact on range and glide.
objectives: Build realistic drag polars from component buildup
Apply to rocket booster reentry or booster return

title: Hypersonic Aerodynamics
summary: Newtonian theory, high-temperature effects, and the extreme environment faced by reentry vehicles and boost-glide weapons.

title: Viscous Interactions & Real Gas
summary: When the boundary layer and shock layer talk to each other at Mach 10+.

title: Applied Aerodynamics for Rockets
summary: Putting it together: ascent loads, base drag, staging aerodynamics, and booster return (Falcon 9, Starship).
